headlines.title: This autism therapy is covered by many insurers. Why does the military restrict it?
headlines.description: TRICARE, the military’s healthcare program, limits coverage of applied behavior analysis, a type of therapy for people with autism. Kristi Cabiao is trying to get the policy changed.

headlines.title: As cancer death rates plunge, clear disparities remain, report says
headlines.description: Black and American Indian/Alaska Native communities have the highest cancer rates of any racial or ethnic group in the U.S.

headlines.title: Summer Travel Checklist: How to Avoid Heat and Food Illnesses
headlines.description: The summer is peak vacation season, and nothing can ruin a trip like a stomach bug or heat exhaustion. NBC’s medical contributor Dr. John Torres joins TODAY with ways to stay healthy and safe during your travels. Tips include planning around the hot temperatures, drinking safe water, trying local pharmacies for prescriptions and more.

headlines.title: The growing struggle of finding caregivers for seniors as the population in the U.S. ages
headlines.description: In four years, people over 65 will outnumber children under 18 for the first time in the U.S. That means many seniors will struggle to find caregivers. NBC News' Kate Snow reports on how many families will feel the strain of needing to care for an elder, both emotionally and financially.

headlines.title: Kick Off July 2026 With Start TODAY’s Fitness Challenge
headlines.description: Stephanie Mansour shares full-body strength training exercises to help you with the Start TODAY July fitness challenge and TODAY nutrition and health expert Joy Bauer share tips for eating well while beating the heat. Joy also shares two healthy no-cook meals: creamy and crunchy chicken salad and a BLT pasta salad!

headlines.title: New Medical Tools and Tests to Ask Your Doctor About
headlines.description: NBC medical reporter Dr. Akshay Syal joins TODAY with ways to take initiative with your health by asking your doctor about the latest tools and tests. He shares what to know about AI-assisted cancer screenings, Shield blood tests and more.

headlines.title: Fitness trainers swear by weighted vests — here are the best ones
headlines.description: Do weighted vests really work? Here's why trainers swear by working out with ones from Omorpho, TRX, Go Ruck and more.

headlines.title: The best walking shoes for women on Amazon
headlines.description: We compiled a list of walking sneakers recommended by experts and NBC Select editors from brands like Nike, Asics and Saucony — all available on Amazon.

headlines.title: Can “super shoes” make you super speedy? Here’s what to know about carbon plate sneakers
headlines.description: Experts explain the benefits of carbon plate running shoes and who should wear them. Plus, learn the difference between super shoes for racing and training.

headlines.title: Is this $178 bra actually life-changing?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: More people than thought may be at risk for red meat allergy caused by ticks
headlines.description: Nearly a quarter of adults in states crawling with lone star ticks are estimated to show signs that they’ve been bitten, the CDC says.

headlines.title: 6-year-old girl bitten by rabid bat while playing outside her Wisconsin home, family says
headlines.description: The girl’s mother said her sons killed the bat with a homemade “Braveheart”-inspired sword. Her daughter is recovering.
